result:
I now have every literal `#ifdef USE_JEMALLOC` / `#ifdef HAVE_BACKTRACE` site mapped to its enclosing function. Here's the full report.

## `#ifdef USE_JEMALLOC` â€” subsystem: **memory allocator (zmalloc / jemalloc integration)**

All 4 occurrences are memory-allocator code:

| File:Line | Enclosing context | What it gates |
|---|---|---|
| `src/debug.c:349` | `mallctl_int()` (new function def) | Whole helper function that calls `je_mallctl()` to read/write a jemalloc integer tuning param â€” only compiled when jemalloc is the allocator |
| `src/debug.c:460` | `DEBUG HELP` text array | The `MALLCTL <key> [<val>]` / `MALLCTL-STR` help-string entries, since those subcommands only exist under jemalloc |
| `src/debug.c:1104` | `debugCommand()` dispatcher | The `mallctl` / `mallctl-str` subcommand branches of `DEBUG`, delegating to `mallctl_int`/`mallctl_string` |
| `src/zmalloc.c:637` | `zfree_with_size()` | Uses `je_sdallocx()` (jemalloc's sized-deallocation fast path) instead of falling back to plain `zfree()` |

Plus, for completeness, `src/zmalloc.h` and `src/zmalloc.c` itself contain many more jemalloc gates written as `#if defined(USE_JEMALLOC)` (e.g. `zmalloc.h:31,82,89,135,141`) rather than literal `#ifdef` â€” same allocator subsystem, just a different directive spelling, so I excluded them from the strict count but flag them since they're the bulk of jemalloc-specific code (allocator selection, defrag hints, usable-size query, arena stats). Other files (`db.c`, `object.c`, `eval.c`, `function_lua.c`, `script.c`, `syscheck.c`, `lazyfree.c`, `server.c`, `sds.c`, `cluster_asm.c`) also use `#if defined(USE_JEMALLOC)` for allocator-stat reporting / Lua-memory-arena tuning / defrag â€” all still memory-allocator subsystem, none use literal `#ifdef`.

## `#ifdef HAVE_BACKTRACE` â€” subsystem: **crash/debug reporting** (all in `src/debug.c`)

| Line | Enclosing context | What it gates |
|---|---|---|
| `35` | top-level, before any function | Includes `<execinfo.h>` and `<ucontext.h>` needed for stack unwinding |
| `1260` | `_serverAssert()` | Calls `logStackTrace()` when a `serverAssert` fails |
| `1381` | `_serverPanic()` | Calls `logStackTrace()` on a panic before generating the crash report |
| `1408` | after `bugReportStart()`, guards the block containing `getAndSetMcontextEip`/`logStackContent`/`logRegisters` etc. | Wraps the mcontext/register-dump helper functions used only when backtrace support exists |
| `2111` | `setupStacktracePipe()` area / `BACKTRACE_MAX_SIZE` def | Defines the pipe used to ship stack traces from signal handlers, plus backtrace buffer size constant |
| `2564` | `sigsegvHandler()` | Captures EIP via `getAndSetMcontextEip`, calls `logStackTrace()` and `logRegisters()` on SIGSEGV/SIGBUS |
| `2598` | `sigsegvHandler()` (same function, later block) | Calls `dumpCodeAroundEIP()` to disassemble around the crash address |
| `2752` | `sigalrmSignalHandler()` (watchdog) | Declares `ucontext_t *uc` used to get the trapped instruction pointer |
| `2766` | `sigalrmSignalHandler()` (same function) | Calls `logStackTrace()` for the watchdog timer / manual `SIGALRM` request; `#else` branch logs "no support for backtrace()" |
| `2818` | end of `debugDelay()`, guarding subsequent stacktrace utility functions | Begins the `Stacktrace Utils` section (Linux-specific helpers for the crash reporter) |

**Summary:** `USE_JEMALLOC` gates are exclusively the memory-allocator subsystem (`zmalloc`/`DEBUG MALLCTL`), letting Redis use jemalloc-specific APIs (`je_mallctl`, `je_sdallocx`) for stats/tuning/fast-free. `HAVE_BACKTRACE` gates are exclusively the crash/debug-reporting subsystem in `debug.c` â€” signal handlers (`sigsegvHandler`, `sigalrmSignalHandler`), assertion/panic paths (`_serverAssert`, `_serverPanic`), and the stack-trace/register-dump infrastructure they rely on.
